0.solution: Uproot and burn infected plants immediately to prevent spread. Control the whitefly vector by spraying Imidacloprid 17.8% SL (0.3ml/L) or Thiamethoxam 25% WG (0.2g/L). Use reflective mulches to repel whiteflies. Plant virus-resistant cotton varieties like CIM-496 or MNH-786. Avoid planting near infected fields.

1.solution: Plant is healthy. Maintain regular scouting for pests. Apply balanced NPK fertilizer (120:60:60 kg/ha). Ensure proper plant spacing (75x30 cm) for airflow. Use drip irrigation to avoid waterlogging.

2.solution: Plant is healthy. Maintain adequate soil moisture especially during pod-filling stage. Apply Rhizobium inoculant before sowing. Avoid waterlogging. Use Chlorpyrifos 20% EC (2ml/L) as preventive spray against pod borers.

3.solution: Citrus greening (HLB) has no cure. Immediately remove and burn all infected trees. Control the Asian citrus psyllid vector by spraying Dimethoate 30% EC (2ml/L) or Imidacloprid 17.8% SL (0.5ml/L) every 21 days. Use certified disease-free nursery plants. Apply micronutrient sprays (Zinc Sulfate 0.5% + Manganese Sulfate 0.3%) to slow symptom progression.

4.solution: Plant is healthy. Apply Zinc Sulfate (0.5%) and Iron Sulfate (0.3%) foliar sprays every 45 days. Maintain proper irrigation scheduling. Use Chlorpyrifos 20% EC as preventive insecticide against psyllids.

5.solution: Apply Propiconazole 25% EC (Tilt) at 1ml/L or Mancozeb 75% WP at 2.5g/L at first sign of orange pustules. Repeat spray after 10-14 days. Avoid excess nitrogen fertilization. Plant rust-resistant hybrids like DKC-9144 or Pioneer 30V92. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops.

6.solution: Plant is healthy. Apply nitrogen side-dressing (60 kg/ha urea) at V6-V8 growth stage. Maintain weed control using Atrazine 50% WP (1.5kg/ha) pre-emergence. Ensure proper drainage to prevent waterlogging.

7.solution: Spray Mancozeb 75% WP (2.5g/L) or Copper Oxychloride 50% WP (3g/L) at 10-15 day intervals starting from flowering. Use Iprodione 50% WP (1g/L) for severe infections. Sow early (October) to avoid peak disease period. Use certified disease-free seeds treated with Thiram 75% WS (3g/kg seed). Avoid overhead irrigation.

8.solution: Plant is healthy. Apply sulfur (40 kg/ha) and phosphorus (60 kg/ha) fertilizers for better pod formation. Use Quinalphos 25% EC (2ml/L) as preventive spray against aphids. Ensure proper row spacing (30 cm) for airflow.

9.solution: Apply Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (Ridomil Gold) 2.5g/L or Cymoxanil 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP (3g/L) immediately upon spotting water-soaked lesions. Spray Chlorothalonil 75% WP (2g/L) as protectant every 7 days in humid weather. Destroy infected haulms before harvest. Store tubers in cool dry conditions. Plant certified blight-free seed tubers.

10.solution: Plant is healthy. Earth up soil around base at 30 days after planting. Apply Mancozeb 75% WP (2g/L) as preventive spray during humid weather. Use certified seed tubers. Practice 3-year crop rotation.

11.solution: Spray Propiconazole 25% EC (1ml/L) or Mancozeb 75% WP + Tricyclazole 75% WP (2g/L each) at booting stage. Improve soil fertility with potassium (60 kg/ha MOP) and nitrogen management. Avoid water stress during tillering. Use resistant varieties like IR-64 or Swarna. Treat seeds with Carbendazim 50% WP (2g/kg) before sowing.

12.solution: Spray Tricyclazole 75% WP (0.6g/L) or Isoprothiolane 40% EC (1.5ml/L) immediately upon spotting diamond-shaped lesions. Avoid excess nitrogen application. Maintain continuous flooding (5 cm) in paddy. Use blast-resistant varieties like Pusa Basmati-1 or IR-64. Treat seeds with Carbendazim 50% WP (2g/kg) before sowing.

13.solution: Plant is healthy. Maintain 2-5 cm standing water throughout vegetative stage. Apply nitrogen in splits (basal + tillering + panicle initiation). Use Chlorpyrifos 20% EC (2ml/L) as preventive spray against stem borers.

14.solution: Highly destructive — act immediately. Burn the entire infected crop in the field. Do NOT ratoon the diseased crop. Disinfect harvesting tools with 0.1% Mercuric Chloride or 10% Bleach solution. Treat setts with Carbendazim 50% WP (1g/L) for 30 minutes before planting. Plant resistant varieties like CoJ-64 or CoS-8436. Avoid waterlogging by improving field drainage.

15.solution: Plant is healthy. Earth up at 60 and 120 days after planting to prevent lodging. Apply Atrazine 50% WP (2kg/ha) for weed control. Use Chlorpyrifos 20% EC (4L/ha) as soil drench against early shoot borers.

16.solution: Remove and destroy all lower infected leaves immediately. Spray Chlorothalonil 75% WP (2g/L) or Mancozeb 75% WP (2.5g/L) on a 7-day schedule. For severe infection use Azoxystrobin 23% SC (1ml/L) or Difenoconazole 25% EC (0.5ml/L). Avoid overhead irrigation. Stake plants to keep foliage off ground. Rotate crops — avoid tomato in same field for 2 years.

17.solution: Plant is healthy. Stake or trellis plants to keep foliage off ground. Use drip irrigation to avoid wetting leaves. Apply Copper Oxychloride 50% WP (3g/L) as preventive spray every 15 days during humid weather.

18.solution: Spray Propiconazole 25% EC (Tilt) at 1ml/L or Tebuconazole 25.9% EC (1ml/L) immediately upon spotting first yellow stripes. Repeat after 15 days if disease persists. Avoid late sowing and excess nitrogen. Plant resistant varieties like HD-2967, PBW-550 or WH-1105. Monitor fields weekly during February-March (peak rust season).

19.solution: Plant is healthy. Apply first irrigation at Crown Root Initiation (CRI) stage (21 days after sowing). Use recommended herbicides like Clodinafop 15% WP (400g/ha) for narrow-leaf weeds. Apply Propiconazole 25% EC (1ml/L) as preventive spray at flag leaf stage.
